code: @Manual{,   title = {riot: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography},   author = {Aymeric Stamm},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/astamm/riot}, }

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot (R Input/Output Tractography) package provides R interface importing exporting tractography data R. Currently supported importing formats : native VTK .vtk .vtp files; medInria .fds files; MRtrix .tck/.tsf files; , TrackVis .trk files. package reads tractography data tibble row point characterized least following five variables: X, Y, Z: 3D coordinates current point; PointId: Identification number current point among points streamline belongs ; StreamlineId: Identification number streamline current point belongs . points might also attributes color assigned , case, additional variables properly created import well. user can perform statistical analysis point cloud store new variable (s)deem useful additional column tibble. package also allows write back tibble, including newly created attributes, following exporting formats: native VTK .vtk .vtp files; , medInria .fds files.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: can install released version riot CRAN : Alternatively can install development version riot GitHub :

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: Since version 1.2.0, riot longer bundles VTK source files. Instead links externally installed VTK (>= 9.1.0). VTK must present host installing package. shared static VTK builds supported; static builds macOS Linux must compiled -fPIC. install time, configure (Unix-like) / configure.win (Windows) search VTK following order: VTK_DIR environment variable (highest priority). Homebrew — macOS (brew install vtk). pkg-config — macOS Linux. Well-known system prefix paths (/usr, /usr/local) — Linux. Rtools45 / UCRT64 pacman package mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-vtk — Windows.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot bundles TinyXML-2. tinyxml2.cpp modified avoid use stdout printf per Writing R Extensions manual recommendations R input/output mechanism writing console.

title: Format and print methods for maf_df objects — print.maf_df
code: # Create a sample maf_df object sample_data <- tibble::tibble(   X = rnorm(100),   Y = rnorm(100),   Z = rnorm(100),   PointId = rep(1:10, each = 10),   StreamlineId = rep(1:10, times = 10) ) class(sample_data) <- c("maf_df", class(sample_data)) format(sample_data) #> ℹ Tractogram with 10 streamlines. #> ℹ Distribution of the number of sampled points per streamline: 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, and 10. print(sample_data) #> ℹ Tractogram with 10 streamlines. #> ℹ Distribution of the number of sampled points per streamline: 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, and 10. #> cli-175-344

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
text: go-function import tractograms R. Based VTK DIPY, currently support eight different formats detailed documentation input argument file.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
text: file string specifying path file containing tractography data. Currently supported files : standard VTK formats .vtk .vtp, medInria .fds format, MRtrix .tck/.tsf format, TrackVis .trk .trx formats, DIPY .dpy format, .fib format. reference_file string specifying path reference image file. required importing .trx, .fib, .dpy files, formats contain spatial information image space. reference image used correctly position tractogram appropriate space. Default NULL.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
text: special tibble (class maf_df) containing tractogram data following columns: X: X coordinate point. Y: Y coordinate point. Z: Z coordinate point. PointId: Identifier point within streamline. StreamlineId: Identifier streamline. tractogram may contain additional columns attributes either points streamlines.

title: Import tractograms into R — read_tractogram
code: uf_left_vtk <- read_tractogram(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk",  package = "riot")) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> ✔ The tractogram stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtk has been successfully imported.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
text: function exports tractogram stored maf_df object file one supported formats. Supported formats include VTK (.vtk), VTP (.vtp), FDS (.fds), TRK (.trk), TCK (.tck), TRX (.trx), FIB (.fib), DPY (.dpy). formats require reference image (TRX, FIB, DPY), user must provide path reference image file.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
text: x object class maf_df storing tractogram. file string specifying path file containing tractography data. Currently supported files : standard VTK formats .vtk .vtp, medInria .fds format, MRtrix .tck/.tsf format, TrackVis .trk .trx formats, DIPY .dpy format, .fib format. reference_file string specifying path reference image file. required importing .trx, .fib, .dpy files, formats contain spatial information image space. reference image used correctly position tractogram appropriate space. Default NULL.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
text: input tractogram (invisibly) function can used pipes.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
text: Warning: rely DIPY provide support save tractograms .trk, .trx, .tck, .dpy .fib formats. Among formats, .trk .trx formats able keep track additional attributes assigned either streamlines points.

title: Export tractograms from R — write_tractogram
code: uf_left  <- read_tractogram(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtp",  package = "riot")) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> v The tractogram stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtp has been successfully imported. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ out <- fs::file_temp(ext = ".vtp") write_tractogram(uf_left, file = out) } # }

title: riot 1.2.0
text: Breaking change system requirements: riot longer bundles VTK source files. Instead, links externally installed VTK (>= 9.1.0) compile time. VTK must present host installing package. shared static VTK builds supported; static builds macOS Linux must compiled -fPIC. user-supplied VTK_DIR environment variable. Homebrew (macOS). pkg-config (macOS Linux). Well-known system include paths (/usr, /usr/local) (Linux). Rtools45 / UCRT64 pacman package mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-vtk (Windows). Windows, VTK loaded dynamically run time via addDLLDirectory() avoid bundle VTK runtime DLLs inside package. Removed bundled VTK source files, reducing installed package size considerably. Updated SystemRequirements DESCRIPTION document external VTK dependency.

title: riot 1.1.1
text: Modified VTK source files avoid compilation warnings arising using LLVM Apple clang GNU gcc compilers. Now ships shrunk version VTK source files avoid unsuccessful downloads VTK website. Fix compilation errors vtkzlib raised clang16.

title: riot 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-01-06 Update VTK v9.2.4; Avoid prototype checks vtkzlib using LLVM Clang compiler.

title: riot 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-10 first major release, : Added support read MRtrix .tck/.tsf file formats (#5). Added support read TrackVis .trk file formats (#5). Use one core compile VTK compliance CRAN policy (thanks Prof. B. Ripley). Added tilde expansion file paths. make first major release consider popular tractography formats now supported riot. chose design support VTK medInria file formats writing.

title: riot 0.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-05 Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Supports reading writing .vtk, .vtp .fds file formats.
